raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.5","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally includes strengthening the thesis, contribution framing, and gap statement at the paper level.","R1.2":"The request implies checking whether the system description, benchmark claims, and conclusions form a coherent argument chain.","R1.3":"The improvement task includes ensuring reported metrics and comparisons support the claims made from them.","R1.4":"The draft makes explicit first-system and prior-work claims, so assessing novelty positioning is central to improving it.","R1.5":"A broad draft-improvement request includes revising section-level narrative order and overall argumentative arc.","R1.6":"Writing polish is load-bearing because improving a draft directly entails sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and concision.","R1.7":"The named prior work and attribution-sensitive novelty claims make citation and scholarship responsibilities central.","RX.1":"Any improvement should disclose uncertainty where claims depend on unverified benchmarks, deployments, or prior-work comparisons.","RX.2":"The draft contains strong firstness and production-scale claims, making overclaim avoidance essential.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within draft improvement rather than executing experiments or inventing unsupported results.","RX.4":"The improvement should avoid changes that could mislead readers about system capability, evaluation validity, or scholarly priority.","RX.5":"Any suggested changes should preserve traceability to the artifact’s stated evidence, metrics, and citations."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally includes strengthening the thesis, contribution framing, and gap statement at the paper level.
rationale.R1.2: The request implies checking whether the system description, benchmark claims, and conclusions form a coherent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: The improvement task includes ensuring reported metrics and comparisons support the claims made from them.
rationale.R1.4: The draft makes explicit first-system and prior-work claims, so assessing novelty positioning is central to improving it.
rationale.R1.5: A broad draft-improvement request includes revising section-level narrative order and overall argumentative arc.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is load-bearing because improving a draft directly entails sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and concision.
rationale.R1.7: The named prior work and attribution-sensitive novelty claims make citation and scholarship responsibilities central.
rationale.RX.1: Any improvement should disclose uncertainty where claims depend on unverified benchmarks, deployments, or prior-work comparisons.
rationale.RX.2: The draft contains strong firstness and production-scale claims, making overclaim avoidance essential.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within draft improvement rather than executing experiments or inventing unsupported results.
rationale.RX.4: The improvement should avoid changes that could mislead readers about system capability, evaluation validity, or scholarly priority.
rationale.RX.5: Any suggested changes should preserve traceability to the artifact’s stated evidence, metrics, and citations.
